framing: Manufacturing Operations Director moving to new company

jd.title: Director of Operations — Automotive Manufacturing

requirements.text: 12+ years in manufacturing operations with at least 5 years in automotive
Experience managing production facilities with 500+ employees
Deep knowledge of lean manufacturing, Six Sigma, and Toyota Production System (TPS)
Experience with EV or electrified powertrain manufacturing is a strong advantage
Track record of achieving OEE improvements of 10%+ year-over-year
Experience with MES (Manufacturing Execution Systems) and SAP PP
MS in Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, or MBA
Fluency in English; German language skills preferred

responsibilities: Oversee daily operations of automotive component manufacturing plant (800 employees)
Drive continuous improvement programs across quality, cost, delivery, and safety KPIs
Manage annual operating budget of EUR 120M and capital investment pipeline
Lead digital transformation initiatives including MES deployment and predictive maintenance
Ensure compliance with IATF 16949, ISO 14001, and German workplace safety regulations
Report plant performance to VP Manufacturing and participate in global operations reviews

expected_output.summary: Manufacturing Operations leader with 14 years of progressive experience across automotive and industrial manufacturing. Currently Plant Director at Continental AG managing 650-employee facility producing electronic brake systems. Delivered OEE improvement from 72% to 86% over 3 years through lean manufacturing and MES implementation. Six Sigma Black Belt with MS Industrial Engineering and EUR 15M capital investment track record.

experience.title: Plant Director — Electronic Brake Systems
bullets: Directed operations of 650-employee manufacturing plant producing electronic brake control units for BMW, VW, and Stellantis
Improved Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) from 72% to 86% through lean manufacturing and TPM programs
Managed annual operating budget of EUR 95M and led EUR 15M capital investment for new SMT production line
Deployed Manufacturing Execution System (MES) integrating SAP PP with shop floor equipment, reducing scrap by 18%
Achieved IATF 16949 recertification with zero major non-conformances for 3 consecutive years

experience.title: Senior Production Manager — Powertrain Components
bullets: Managed 3 production lines (280 employees) manufacturing fuel injection systems and turbocharger components
Led Six Sigma improvement projects delivering EUR 2.4M annual cost savings
Implemented predictive maintenance program reducing unplanned downtime by 32%
Coordinated with R&D on NPI (New Product Introduction) for electrified powertrain components

experience.title: Lean Specialist (promoted from Production Engineer, 2014)
bullets: Promoted from production engineer to lean specialist in industrial automation manufacturing
Facilitated 25+ Kaizen events and trained 100+ operators on lean manufacturing principles
Supported SAP PP implementation for production planning and shop floor data collection

certifications: Lean Practitioner — SME (Society of Manufacturing Engineers)
education: MS Industrial Engineering — RWTH Aachen University (2009-2011)
BS Mechanical Engineering — Technical University of Munich (2005-2009)

deliberate_flaws: No direct EV/electrified manufacturing experience (only NPI coordination for electrified components)
Budget EUR 95M vs required EUR 120M scope
OEE improvement 14pp over 3 years (~4.7pp/yr) does not meet JD's 10%+ YoY requirement
